title: How usage requirements propagate transitively
description: INTERFACE and PUBLIC properties on a target flow to anything that links it, so consumers inherit includes, definitions, and dependencies automatically.
body: Use when reasoning about why a consumer compiles without restating a dependency's settings. Do not assume each target must re-declare what it links. A target's INTERFACE_* properties attach to consumers at link time, and PUBLIC merges INTERFACE with the build-time PRIVATE effect; chains of links propagate these transitively. Inspect with the target's INTERFACE properties to see what consumers receive. Validation signals include a deep consumer building correctly through several link layers. Risk boundary: marking implementation deps PUBLIC leaks them down the whole chain, so scope visibility carefully.
